plan.summary: Implement a dark mode feature for the website, allowing users to toggle between light and dark themes and persisting their preference across sessions. This will involve defining CSS variables for colors, creating a theme toggle UI, and adding JavaScript logic to manage theme switching and persistence.

steps.description: Analyze existing CSS structure to identify primary colors, backgrounds, and text colors that need to be themed. Determine the best approach for defining theme-specific styles (e.g., using CSS variables with a `data-theme` attribute on the `html` or `body` element).
steps.details: Review `style.css` (or equivalent main stylesheet) to list all color-related properties. Decide if `html[data-theme='dark']` or `body.dark-mode` will be used for theme application.

steps.description: Define CSS variables for all relevant colors (backgrounds, text, primary, secondary, borders, etc.) in both light and dark themes. These variables will be used throughout the stylesheet.
steps.details: Modify `style.css` (or create a new `_variables.css` file if using a preprocessor) to define `:root` variables for the light theme and override them within a `[data-theme='dark']` selector for the dark theme. Update existing styles to use these new CSS variables.

steps.description: Add a theme toggle button or switch to the website's UI (e.g., in the header or navigation bar). This element will trigger the theme change.
steps.details: Modify `index.html` (or your main layout file, e.g., `header.html` or `nav.html`) to include a button or switch element with a unique ID, for example, `id="theme-toggle"`.

steps.description: Implement JavaScript logic to handle theme switching. This includes detecting the user's system preference, reading/writing theme preference to local storage, and applying the correct theme class/attribute to the `html` element.
steps.details:
Modify `script.js` (or create a new `theme.js` file) to:
1. Get the theme toggle button and the `html` element.
2. Define a function to apply a theme (e.g., `applyTheme('dark')` which sets `html.setAttribute('data-theme', 'dark')` and saves to `localStorage`).
3. On page load, check `localStorage` for a saved theme preference. If none, check `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')`.
4. Apply the determined theme.
5. Add an event listener to the theme toggle button to switch between 'light' and 'dark' themes and update `localStorage`.

steps.description: Thoroughly test the dark mode implementation across different browsers and devices. Verify that all UI elements are correctly styled in both light and dark modes, the toggle works, and the preference persists.
steps.details: Test on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge. Check responsiveness on mobile and tablet. Verify contrast ratios for accessibility. Test initial load with system preference, then toggle, then refresh to ensure persistence. Pay attention to images, icons, and third-party components.

steps.description: Deploy the changes to a staging environment for final review, then to production.
steps.details: Push code to version control, deploy to staging, perform final QA, then deploy to production environment.

risks: Incomplete styling: Some UI elements or third-party components might not be correctly themed, leading to visual inconsistencies.
Accessibility issues: Insufficient contrast between text and background colors in either theme could make content unreadable for some users.
Flickering on page load: If JavaScript applies the theme after the initial render, a brief 'flash' of the default theme might occur before the correct theme is loaded.
Performance impact: While minimal, adding more CSS variables and JS logic can slightly increase page load time.
plan.rollback_strategy: Revert the changes in `index.html`, `css/style.css`, and `js/script.js` to their previous versions using version control (e.g., Git). This will remove the dark mode functionality and restore the website to its original state.
plan.success_criteria: The user can click a toggle button to switch between light and dark themes. The chosen theme is applied consistently across all website pages and persists when the user revisits the site. All text, backgrounds, and interactive elements maintain appropriate contrast and visual appeal in both themes.
